elements.sent-2411-001: This work is devoted to validating the results obtained using the Monte Carlo generator {\tt ReneSANCe .}
elements.sent-2411-002: A comparison of differential cross sections, as well as single- and double-spin asymmetries, taking into account the polarization of initial states is presented.
elements.sent-2411-003: Complete one-loop electroweak radiative corrections to the charged-current Drell-Yan processes were presented for the case of longitudinal polarization of initial particles in the previous work \cite{Bondarenko:2024xzl .}
elements.sent-2411-004: This research contributes to a global next-to-leading order analysis of polarized parton distributions in proton-proton collisions at {\tt RHIC \cite{RHICSPIN:2023zxx .}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Previously, a tuned comparison of the codes {\tt HORACE \cite{CarloniCalame:2003ux,CarloniCalame:2006zq , {\tt WGRAD2 \cite{Baur:1998kt and { \tt SANC \cite{Arbuzov:2005dd for the unpolarized case was performed and a good agreement was found (see section 4.4 of the proceedings \cite{TeV4LHC-Top:2007fwh ).}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Also, in paper \cite{Bondarenko:2024xzl an agreement was also obtained at leading order with the works \cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm except in the polarized case.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: In this brief note, we present the results of a comparison for polarized case with those from works \cite{Gullenstern:1996pw and with \cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm .}}
elements.sent-2411-008: A comparison of the components of the polarized differential cross section with those obtained using the {\tt SPHINX program~\cite{Gullenstern:1996pw , as well as of the single- and double-spin asymmetries in~\cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm , is presented.}}}
elements.sent-2411-009: It is important to note that the comparison was performed at the leading order and a good agreement was found.
elements.sent-2411-010: At the one-loop level, the works~\cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm do not include the subtraction of quark mass singularities.}
elements.sent-2411-011: Therefore, no comparison is possible in this case.
elements.sent-2411-012: Numerical results are obtained using the Monte-Carlo generator {\tt ReneSANCe \cite{Bondarenko:2022mbi .}}
elements.sent-2411-013: The paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-014: In Section \ref{section1 the basic formulae for differential cross section and the polarized observables are given.}
elements.sent-2411-015: Section \ref{section3 is devoted to the presentation and discussion of the numerical results.}
elements.sent-2411-016: Our conclusions are drawn in Section \ref{Conclusion .}
elements.sent-2411-017: Here, we present the basic formulae of calculating differential cross sections at one loop level.
elements.sent-2411-018: They take the following form at the hadronic level: \begin{eqnarray}pp \to W^{+(-)} + X \to \ell^{+(-)}\nu_\ell(\bar{\nu}_\ell) + X, \label{DYCC}\end{eqnarray} with $\ell= e,\mu$.
elements.sent-2411-019: The differential cross section is obtained by convolution with the corresponding parton distribution functions: \begin{eqnarray}\label{sigpp} d\sigma(\Lambda_1,\Lambda_2,s) = \sum_{q_1 q_2}\sum_{\lambda_1\lambda_2}\int_{0}^{1}\int_{0}^{1} dx_1 dx_2 {f}_{q_1}^{\Lambda_1\lambda_1}(x_1) \times {f}_{q_2}^{\Lambda_2\lambda_2}(x_2)\, d\hat{\sigma}_{q_1 q_2}(\lambda_1,\lambda_2,\hat{s}), \nonumber\end{eqnarray} where $\Lambda_i = \pm 1$ and $\lambda_i = \pm 1$ are the helicities of each proton and quark, respectively, with $\hat{s = x_1 x_2s$.}
elements.sent-2411-020: Parton distributions ${f _{q_i ^{\Lambda_i\lambda_i}$ can be derived using unpolarized $f_{q_i}$ and longitudinally polarized $\Delta f_{q_i}$ PDFs: ${f _{q_i ^{\Lambda_i\lambda_i = \dfrac{1 {2 ( f_{q_i + \Lambda_i\lambda_i\Delta f_{q_i ).$ To derive longitudinally polarized cross sections, helicity amplitudes are calculated, and equation~(1.15) from~\cite{MoortgatPick:2005cw is used.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: At the partonic level, reactions take the following form: \begin{equation} \bar{d}(p_1,\lambda_1) + {u}(p_2,\lambda_2) \to l^+(p_3,\lambda_3) + \nu_l(p_4,\lambda_4)~(+ \gamma(p_5,\lambda_5)), \bar{u}(p_1,\lambda_1) + {d}(p_2,\lambda_2)\to {l}^-(p_3,\lambda_3) + \bar\nu_l(p_4,\lambda_4)~(+ \gamma(p_5,\lambda_5)), \end{equation} where $p_i$ is momenta and $\lambda_i$ is helicity.
elements.sent-2411-022: As usual, the differential cross section is subdivided into five parts, as follows: \begin{eqnarray}\hat{\sigma}^{\text{1-loop}} = \hat{\sigma}^{\mathrm{Born}} + \hat{\sigma}^{\mathrm{virt}}(\lambda) + \hat{\sigma}^{\mathrm{soft}}(\lambda, \omega) + \hat{\sigma}^{\mathrm{hard}}(\omega) + \hat{\sigma}^{\mathrm{Subt}}, \label{loopxsec}\end{eqnarray} where $\hat{\sigma ^{\mathrm{Born }$ represents the contribution from the Born cross section, $\hat{\sigma ^{\mathrm{virt }$ accounts for the virtual (loop) corrections, $\hat{\sigma ^{\mathrm{soft }$ corresponds to the soft photon emission, and $\hat{\sigma ^{\mathrm{hard }$ refers to the hard photon emission (with energy $E_{\gamma > \omega$).}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: Last two terms are defined using the soft-hard separator $\omega$, along with the auxiliary parameter $\lambda$ (a fictitious {\it photon mass that regularizes infrared divergences).}
elements.sent-2411-024: The special term $\hat{\sigma ^{\mathrm{Subt }$ denotes the subtraction of collinear quark mass singularities.}}
elements.sent-2411-025: When all contributions to the cross section are summed, the result is free of infrared divergences.
elements.sent-2411-026: The partonic cross section is evaluated in the center-of-mass system of the initial quarks/antiquarks.
elements.sent-2411-027: All contributions are obtained using the helicity amplitudes approach, with a sum over the helicities of all final state particles.
elements.sent-2411-028: The differential cross sections, along with the single- and double-spin asymmetries, are compared to alternative codes.
elements.sent-2411-029: Here, some details of this comparison are discussed.
elements.sent-2411-030: As mentioned in the Introduction, the comparison of the polarized differential cross section is carried out using the {\tt SPHINX program.}
elements.sent-2411-031: We compare the following components: $\sigma^{00}$, $\sigma^{++}$, $\sigma^{+-}$, $\sigma^{-+}$, $\sigma^{--}$, where $\sigma^{00 =\dfrac{1 {4}\left(\sigma^{++ +\sigma^{+- +\sigma^{-+ +\sigma^{--}\right)$ is unpolarized cross section.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-032: To define asymmetries, we introduce the following combinations of polarized components: \begin{eqnarray}\Delta\sigma_{\mathrm {L}} = \frac{1}{4}\left(\sigma^{++}+\sigma^{+-}-\sigma^{-+}-\sigma^{--}\right), \Delta\sigma_{\mathrm {LL}} = \frac{1}{4}\left(\sigma^{++}-\sigma^{+-}-\sigma^{-+}+\sigma^{--}\right).\end{eqnarray}.
elements.sent-2411-033: Based on these combinations, the single-spin asymmetry ${A _{\mathrm{L (\mathcal{O )$ and the double-spin asymmetry ${A _{\mathrm{LL (\mathcal{O )$ are defined as follows: \begin{eqnarray}\label{AL}\label{ALL} {\text{A}}_{\mathrm{L}(\mathrm{LL})}(\mathcal{O}) = \dfrac{{ d(\Delta\sigma_{\mathrm {L}(\mathrm{LL})})}/{\ds d \mathcal{O}}} {{\ds d\sigma}/{\ds d \mathcal{O}}},\end{eqnarray} where $\mathcal{O}$ is some observable.}}}}}}}}
elements.formula-2411-006: \begin{equation}\label{AL}\label{ALL} {A}_{L(LL)}(O) = \dfrac{{ d(\Delta\sigma_{\mathrm {L}(LL)})}/{\ds d O}} {{\ds d\sigma}/{\ds d O}},\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-034: The $z$ axis is directed along the momentum of the first proton.
elements.sent-2411-035: In the papers \cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm , the same conventions for beam polarization as in {\tt SPHINX are used (relative to a $z^+$ axes direction).}}
elements.figure-2411-001: \begin{tabular}{cc} \includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{LO_crosssection_CC_antimu_nu_500GeV_RS_vs_SPHINX.pdf} \hspace*{0mm} \hspace*{0mm}\includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{LO_crosssection_CC_mu_nu_500GeV_RS_vs_SPHINX.pdf} \end{tabular} \caption{Results of the comparison differential cross sections as a function of rapidity for both W$^{+}$ and W$^{-}$ channels between {\tt ReneSANCe} and {\tt SPHINX}.\label{fig:CS}}
elements.figure-2411-002: \begin{tabular}{cc} \includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{AL_CC_antimu_Zykunov_500GeV_minus.pdf} \hspace*{0mm} \hspace*{0mm}\includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{AL_CC_mu_Zykunov_500GeV_minus.pdf} \end{tabular} \caption{Results for single-spin asymmetries as a function of transverse momentum for different values of pseudo-rapidity $\eta$.\label{fig:AL}}
elements.figure-2411-003: \begin{tabular}{cc} \includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{ALL_CC_antimu_Zykunov_500GeV_minus.pdf} \hspace*{0mm} \hspace*{0mm}\includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{ALL_CC_mu_Zykunov_500GeV_minus.pdf} \end{tabular} \caption{Results for double-spin asymmetries as a function of transverse momentum for different values of pseudo-rapidity $\eta$.\label{fig:ALL}}
elements.sent-2411-036: In this section we present the results of our comparison of differential cross sections between {\tt ReneSANCe and {\tt SPHINX \cite{Gullenstern:1996pw and for single- and double-asymmetries with works \cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-037: For a comparison, the set of input parameters is used as in \cite{Bondarenko:2024xzl .}
elements.sent-2411-039: We use the PDF set {\tt NNPDF23\_nlo\_as\_0119 for unpolarized $f_{q_i}$ and the PDF set {\tt NNPDFpol11\_100 for longitudinally polarized $\Delta f_{q_i}$ parton distribution functions from the {\tt LHAPDF6 library, using a factorization scale of $\mu_F = M_{\ell\ell}$~\cite{ParticleDataGroup:2020ssz .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-040: Figure \ref{fig:CS illustrates the differential cross sections as functions of rapidity $Y_{W^{\pm }$.}}
elements.sent-2411-041: In this Figure, the results of {\tt ReneSANCe are represented as curves without markers, whereas those of {\tt SPHINX are shown with markers.}}
elements.sent-2411-042: As shown in Figure 1, the two codes show complete agreement across all combinations of initial state polarizations.
elements.sent-2411-043: To compare asymmetries with those presented in works \cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm , we use the set of input parameters and kinematic cuts as described in these studies.}
elements.sent-2411-044: However, some values of the input parameters used to obtain the results do not completely match those provided in the articles, as, for example, the width of $W$-boson and the sine of the Weinberg angle are not specified.
elements.sent-2411-045: In Figures \ref{fig:AL and \ref{fig:ALL , the results for single- and double-spin asymmetries are presented.}}
elements.sent-2411-046: The $W^{+}$-channel is presented on the left, while the $W^{-}$-channel is shown on the right.
elements.sent-2411-047: These asymmetries are shown as function of transverse momentum for pseudo-rapidity values $-1$, 0, 1, and 2, which correspond to curves 1, 2, 3, and 5 from the \cite{Zykunov:2003gm .}
elements.sent-2411-048: To compare the results it is necessary to consider the dashed curves from the work \cite{Zykunov:2003gm .}
elements.sent-2411-049: A good agreement has been reached between {\tt ReneSANCe and the mentioned above articles, despite the differences in some input parameters.}
elements.sent-2411-050: The polarized charged-current Drell-Yan process has been implemented in the Monte-Carlo generator {\tt ReneSANCe .}
elements.sent-2411-051: The distributions for single- and double-spin asymmetries were thoroughly compared with the results of the papers \cite{Zykunov:2001mn,Zykunov:2003gm and the {\tt SPHINX program and excellent agreement was obtained at the Born level.}}
elements.sent-2411-052: For the one-loop level, we obtained a result without double counting and independent of the quark masses.
elements.sent-2411-053: We have shown that the effect of radiative corrections on single- and double-spin asymmetries is negligible against the uncertainties of available polarized parton distribution functions.
